intent: Redesign the upload dialog button colors across dark, light, and purple modes before any local file has been selected.
assumptions: The empty state is uploadFiles.length === 0, surfaced as data-ready-to-upload="false" on the footer.
Select files is the primary empty-state local action.
Footer Attach files remains disabled until files are queued, but should look intentionally inactive rather than like a broken primary button.
URL Upload belongs to the remote URL panel and needs a separate secondary action treatment.
risks: Reusing the generic primary button class for all upload actions makes future color changes bleed across unrelated buttons.
Disabled states can become too bright and look clickable.
Theme-specific hover and disabled declarations can silently fail if they use undefined CSS tokens.
steps: Add failing tests requiring semantic button classes and empty-state palette tokens.
Add semantic classes for cancel, attach, select files, and URL upload.
Define dark, light, and purple empty-state palette variables in UploadDialog.css.
Apply scoped empty-state footer styles and standalone select/URL styles.
Capture visual evidence for dark, light, and purple empty states.
Run focused test, lint, build, and whitespace checks.
